# Contract: Diagnostic Report

**Implementation**: `notes/2026-05-04-phase1-diagnostic.md` (handwritten by the maintainer during the diagnostic; not generated by a script).
**Schema source**: data-model.md Entity 10 (Diagnostic report)
**Spec reference**: FR-006, FR-007, FR-008, FR-009, FR-011, FR-012, FR-013, FR-014, US4

## Purpose

The single permanent record of the Phase 1 diagnostic. Every artifact, every
state YAML, every run-log entry, every iteration diff, every defect identified,
and every fix applied gets quoted here verbatim. The report is the
authoritative input for: (a) the carry-forward gate (US5), (b) issue #107's
closeout, (c) follow-up issues for deferred defects, (d) future-phase
diagnostics that need to know what Phase 1 actually produced.

## File path

`notes/2026-05-04-phase1-diagnostic.md` — fixed. The `2026-05-04` prefix
matches the date this work began. If the diagnostic spans multiple days,
the prefix does NOT change (the date is metadata, not content).

## Required structure

### Section 1: Header

```markdown
# Phase 1 (Idea Lifecycle) Diagnostic Report

**Date range**: <YYYY-MM-DD> – <YYYY-MM-DD>
**Spec**: [specs/003-phase1-idea-lifecycle-testing/spec.md](../specs/003-phase1-idea-lifecycle-testing/spec.md)
**Tracker**: [#107](https://github.com/ContextLab/llmXive/issues/107)
**Branch**: `003-phase1-idea-lifecycle-testing`
**Final commit at report time**: `<short-sha>`
**Backend**: Dartmouth Chat (`is_paid: false` per agents/registry.yaml)
```

### Section 2: Executive summary

≤ 1 page. Three subsections:

- **What worked well** (3-5 bullets, each citing a specific quoted artifact)
- **What needs improvement** (3-5 bullets, severity tag inline)
- **What's broken** (CRITICAL/HIGH defects only, each with file:line pointer)

End with a one-sentence carry-forward verdict: "N projects carried forward to spec 004 (see Section 7 / `carry-forward.yaml`)."

### Section 3: Per-agent runs

One subsection per agent, each containing one nested subsection per cohort/project tested. Required content per agent subsection:

```markdown
## Brainstorm — Cohort 1 (commit <sha>)

**Prompt version under test**: <prompt_version> (`agents/prompts/brainstorm.md` at <sha>)
**Backend / model**: dartmouth / google.gemma-3-27b-it (per registry default)
**Cohort members** (8 projects):

1. PROJ-NNN-<slug-1>
2. ...
8. PROJ-NNN-<slug-8>

### State YAML before & after (per project)

[Quote `state/projects/PROJ-NNN-<slug>.yaml` from immediately before the run, then immediately after, for at least the first 3 cohort members. For brevity, members 4-8 may be summarized in a table.]

### Idea artifact (per project)

[Quote `projects/PROJ-NNN-<slug>/idea/<slug>.md` verbatim for each cohort member. For files >100 lines, truncate per FR-013 with `[truncated lines N-M, sha256: <hash>]`.]

### Run-log entries (per project)

[Quote the relevant JSONL line(s) from `state/run-log/<YYYY-MM>/<run-id>.jsonl`.]

### Acceptance-criteria evaluation (issue #59)

| # | Criterion | PROJ-...-1 | PROJ-...-2 | ... |
|-|-|-|-|-|
| 1 | Outputs a seed.md / idea.md with non-empty title + field + idea body | PASS | PASS | ... |
| 2 | The idea is in scope for 8h GHA cron (no special hardware, no datasets > 1GB) | ... | ... | ... |
| 3 | Does NOT make claims about prior work | ... | ... | ... |
| 4 | Stays under wall_clock_budget_seconds=300 | ... | ... | ... |

[Plus an additional column for the FR-003a scope filter (in-scope idea types only).]

### Cohort defects identified

- **D1** (HIGH, scope_violation): 3 of 8 seeds proposed wet-lab data collection. Prompt patch needed in `agents/prompts/brainstorm.md` to tighten "out of scope" enumeration. → leads to Cohort 2.
- **D2** (MEDIUM, vagueness): 2 of 8 seeds had research questions phrased as imperatives ("Build X") rather than questions ("How does X behave under Y?"). → leads to Cohort 2 prompt tweak.
```

### Section 4: Citation resolution audit

Required per project that ran flesh_out:

```markdown
## Citation resolution — PROJ-NNN-<slug> (commit <sha>)

### Stage 1 (script) output

[Quote the full stdout JSON from `python tests/phase1/citation_resolver.py projects/PROJ-NNN-<slug>/idea/<slug>.md`.]

### Stage 2 (agent verifier) output

[For each citation flagged `ambiguous` in Stage 1, quote the agent verifier's verdict and reasoning.]

### Final per-citation verdicts

| # | Citation (truncated) | Stage 1 | Stage 2 | Final |
|-|-|-|-|-|
| 1 | [Attention Is All You Need (2017)](arxiv:1706.03762) | resolved | n/a | verified |
| 2 | [Some DOI paper (2023)](doi:10.1xxx/yyy) | ambiguous | verified | verified |
| 3 | [Made-up paper (2026)](url:invalid.example) | unreachable | n/a | failed |
```

### Section 5: Iteration diffs

One block per iteration cycle:

````markdown
## Iteration: brainstorm prompt patch (cohort 1 → cohort 2)

**Patch commit**: <short-sha>
**Defect addressed**: D1 (3/8 seeds proposed wet-lab data collection — see Cohort 1)

```diff
diff --git a/agents/prompts/brainstorm.md b/agents/prompts/brainstorm.md
index abc123..def456 100644
--- a/agents/prompts/brainstorm.md
+++ b/agents/prompts/brainstorm.md
@@ -50,5 +50,8 @@
 ## SCOPE CONSTRAINTS (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
 ...
+- **No wet-lab or human-subject data collection.** Re-analyses of pre-existing
+  public datasets are encouraged; new data collection is rejected outright.
```

**Effect on cohort 2**: 0/8 seeds proposed wet-lab data collection (down from 3/8). D1 → resolved.
````

### Section 6: Defects categorized by severity

Single table near the end, in priority order:

```markdown
## Defects summary

| ID | Severity | Category | Description | File:line | Status |
|-|-|-|-|-|-|
| D1 | HIGH | scope_violation | Brainstorm proposes wet-lab data collection | agents/prompts/brainstorm.md:55 | resolved (commit <sha>) |
| D2 | MEDIUM | vagueness | Research questions phrased as imperatives | agents/prompts/brainstorm.md:42 | resolved (commit <sha>) |
| D3 | CRITICAL | hallucinated_citation | flesh_out invented arXiv ID 2410.99999 | agents/prompts/flesh_out.md:21 | deferred (issue #NNN) |
| D4 | LOW | rationale_brevity | idea_selector rationales are 1 sentence | agents/prompts/idea_selector.md:15 | accepted (not addressed in this PR) |
```

### Section 7: After-fix re-runs

For every defect with `Status: resolved (commit <sha>)`, an "after fix" subsection MUST quote the corresponding sibling-iteration artifact + run-log + acceptance-criteria evaluation showing the defect is gone.

### Section 8: Carry-forward summary

Cross-references `specs/003-phase1-idea-lifecycle-testing/carry-forward.yaml`. Quote the YAML verbatim, then add a one-paragraph commentary per project explaining why it was selected.

## Truncation rules (FR-013)

For any quoted file >100 lines:

````markdown
```markdown
<lines 1-50>
[truncated lines 51-180, sha256: <full-file-sha256>]
<lines 181-end>
```
````

The 50/end split is a guideline; pick the cut points that show the most informative parts (typically: header + first interesting section + closing).

## Verbatim discipline (FR-006, FR-007)

- Run-log JSON entries: copy-paste the exact line, preserved as a single physical line in the report's code block (do not pretty-print).
- State YAML: copy-paste the file contents into the code block; do not normalize whitespace or key ordering.
- Idea artifacts: copy-paste verbatim, preserving any unusual spacing or non-ASCII characters.
- Iteration diffs: produced by `git diff` directly; do not edit the diff text.

## What this contract does NOT require

- The report does NOT need to be regenerable from a script. It's a curated narrative document, hand-written by the maintainer.
- The report does NOT need to use any specific Markdown linter / formatter.
- The report does NOT need a TOC (the GitHub renderer can produce one if needed).
